title: Race-held-out ordered-categorical stack with purge-aware validation
summary: CatBoost’s ordered boosting was built to reduce prediction shift from categorical leakage, which is highly relevant here because Driver/Race/Compound interactions are high-cardinality and repeated within race sequences.
rationale: CatBoost’s ordered boosting was built to reduce prediction shift from categorical leakage, which is highly relevant here because Driver/Race/Compound interactions are high-cardinality and repeated within race sequences. This is also the cleanest way to stress-test whether current CV is inflated by same-race future context.
implementation_hint: Train a CatBoost classifier using raw categoricals plus existing numeric features, but generate all meta-features and stacks with whole-race `GroupKFold` only; add an embargo by dropping neighboring laps from training when validating a race if you build any race-level encodings. Blend CatBoost and the tree baseline only from OOF predictions.
expected_effect: May recover signal from high-cardinality categorical combinations without manual frequency engineering and improve generalization if current encodings leak subtle same-race future information.
risk: If the leaderboard/test distribution is close to train races, stricter grouped validation may lower local CV while actually being the more trustworthy estimate; stacking must stay fully OOF.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
